name: Brown Rice Casserole
recipeIngredient: 6 ounces Grated cheese (Jack, Muenster, Cheddar or Gouda)
recipeInstructions:
1. Cook the rice.
2. Set the tofu on a slanted board or pan to drain and prepare the vegetables.
3. Chop the onion, carrots, celery, pepper, and zucchini into pieces about ½-inch square. Quarter mushrooms if they are small, and cut them into sixths or eighths if they are large. Cut the tofu into ½-inch cubes.
4. Heat olive oil and butter in a pan, then fry the onion over medium heat until it is lightly browned, about 5 minutes.
5. Add garlic, nutritional yeast (if using), cumin, and salt. Stir until blended and cook for 1 minute.
6. Add carrots, celery, green pepper, and ½ cup of liquid (mushroom broth or vegetable stock, or water). Cover the pan and braise the vegetables until they begin to soften, about 5 minutes.
7. Add zucchini and mushrooms, then cook for an additional 7-10 minutes. The vegetables should be nearly, but not completely cooked. If the pan gets dry while cooking, add a little more liquid.
8. Combine the vegetables with rice and cheese in a casserole dish lightly oiled with olive oil. Season with salt and plenty of freshly ground black pepper.
9. Gently mix in the tofu cubes. Add a little more liquid to moisten.
10. Cover the dish with foil and bake at 350°F for ½ hour.
11. Remove foil and bake an additional 15 minutes.
12. Garnish with fresh herbs (Parsley or Cilantro, Thyme, Marjoram).
